assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response mentions spot preemptions and monitoring for them during upgrades, explaining the interaction with upgrade processes

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response discusses workload resilience and mentions that spot workloads should be designed to handle disruptions

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends lower surge settings for spot pools (maxSurge=1) rather than higher surge settings

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response consistently shows maxUnavailable=0 for both regular and spot pools, not >0 as expected

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss the potential issue of spot VM availability affecting surge node creation during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes PDB configuration examples and mentions verifying PDBs are in place for all workloads

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands for configuring surge settings including for spot pools

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response recommends the opposite - upgrading on-demand pools first and spot pools second, stating on-demand are more stable
